step_rationale: T-type Ca channel involved in platelet signaling; peripheral role.
mechanism: CACNA1I encodes a T-type calcium channel contributing to platelet activation signaling, though not a primary thrombotic driver in Type 1 MI.

references.title: Ca3.2 T-type calcium channel regulates mouse platelet activation and arterial thrombosis.
references.journal: Journal of thrombosis and haemostasis : JTH
